name: [XS] rename-fake-notifier-bookings-param

name: [M] profile-children-patterns-parks-telegram
description:
## Parent
[L] multi-profile

## What to build
Create the profile_patterns, profile_parks, and profile_telegram_ids child tables with CASCADE deletes. Extend ProfileRepo with child CRUD methods. Extend the Profile domain model to load children. Add CLI commands for editing profile children and managing telegram IDs per profile.

Schema:
- profile_patterns (id, profile_id, pattern TEXT, sort_order)
- profile_parks (id, profile_id, park_query TEXT, map_query TEXT)
- profile_telegram_ids (id, profile_id, tg_id INTEGER) — replaces the old global tg_allowed_ids in profile.json

ProfileRepo extended with: add_pattern, remove_pattern, add_park, remove_park, add_tg_id, remove_tg_id, list_patterns, list_parks, list_tg_ids.

CLI: profile edit <name> for adding/removing parks, patterns. profile tg-add <name> <tg_id>, tg-rm <name> <tg_id>, tg-list <name>. Remove old telegram allow/revoke/list commands that read profile.json directly.

Profile.create is now fully interactive: prompts for parks, patterns, and telegram IDs after creating the profile.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] profile_patterns, profile_parks, profile_telegram_ids tables created with CASCADE deletes
- [ ] Profile model extended to load children from DB
- [ ] ProfileRepo extended with child CRUD methods
- [ ] SqliteStore implements all child methods
- [ ] profile edit <name> lets user add/remove parks, patterns, telegram IDs
- [ ] profile tg-add, tg-rm, tg-list commands for per-profile telegram management
- [ ] Old telegram allow/revoke/list commands removed
- [ ] profile create <name> is fully interactive (prompts for config)
- [ ] profile delete <name> cascades to all child rows
- [ ] Unit tests for child CRUD with in-memory SQLite
- [ ] Cascade delete tests
- [ ] CLI integration tests

## Blocked by
Core profile repo: m15ten15
result: Added profile_patterns/profile_parks/profile_telegram_ids tables with CASCADE FKs and per-connection PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON. Extended ProfileRepo with 9 child CRUD methods (add/remove/list for each table). Moved PatternSpec + parse_pattern + _WEEKDAYS into domain/models.py (application/profile.py now imports FROM domain). Added ParkQuery value object. Profile is fully-hydrated — get_by_name/list_all/list_enabled populate patterns/parks/tg_allowed_ids via N+1 loader. CLI: removed old telegram allow/revoke/list; added profile tg-add/rm/list, profile edit submenu; profile create is now fully interactive (prompts for parks/patterns/tg_ids after core fields). Tests: 65 new + 148 retained = 213 passed. Reviewer-fast PASS with 3 cosmetic tech-debt nits filed. 8 files changed, +843/-308 lines. Commit: aee30ce.

commit.message: feat: profile child tables (patterns, parks, telegram IDs)

name: [XS] profile-children-followups
description:
tech-debt from profile-children review (7e85bx3w):

1. cli.py:112,116 — orphan telegram_app Typer group declared but all command handlers removed. Delete the 2 lines + add_typer. campcli telegram --help currently surfaces an empty stub group.

2. tests/test_profile_repo.py — add test_add_tg_id_duplicate_behavior. Pick a direction: either add UNIQUE(profile_id, tg_id) constraint to profile_telegram_ids schema in store.py:46-50 and assert IntegrityError, OR add doc-test asserting current 'no-op no-error' behavior.

3. store.py:201-230 (ProfileRepo.update) — currently stamps updated_at and constructs a returned Profile even when WHERE matched zero rows. Gate Profile return on cursor.rowcount > 0 OR document update() as best-effort.

Tests must still pass: `.venv/bin/pytest`.

name: [M] mark-not-interested
description:
Let a profile mark a specific park+date-range suggestion as not-interested so it is never suggested again. Persisted in SQLite (profile_not_interested table). Telegram bot handler: reply /not-interested to a notification message resolves the profile from the chat context, locates the suggestion, and records a not-interested row. CLI commands for manual add/remove/list of not-interested entries. Poller filters out not-interested combinations during SearchNotifier processing.
Grilled: no

name: [S] profile-aware-cli-search
description:
## Parent
[L] multi-profile

## What to build
Make CLI search and check commands profile-aware. Add --profile option to search and check commands. Implement default profile logic: when exactly one enabled profile exists, omit --profile requirement. Add profile search <name> command for one-off profile-specific manual searches.

The search command currently loads a single profile from profile.json. After this slice, it loads profiles from ProfileRepo:
- profile search weekend-only: runs search against that profile's parks and patterns
- campcli search --profile weekend-only: same behavior as explicit command
- campcli search (no --profile, 1 enabled profile): auto-selects that profile
- campcli search (no --profile, 0 or 2+ enabled profiles): error with guidance

The check command follows the same rules.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] profile search <name> command runs search for named profile
- [ ] campcli search --profile <name> works on the general search command
- [ ] campcli check --profile <name> works on the check command
- [ ] Default profile: single enabled profile → auto-selected, no error
- [ ] No enabled profiles → error: 'no enabled profiles found'
- [ ] Multiple enabled profiles → error: 'specify --profile'
- [ ] search command uses profile's allowed parks, patterns, horizon, drive-time
- [ ] Integration tests for each case above

## Blocked by
Poller + migration: hb19863q

name: [M] multi-profile-poller-and-migration
description:
## Parent
[L] multi-profile

## What to build
Refactor the Poller and daemon to support multiple profiles with crawl dedup. Add profile.json → DB migration.

Poller refactor:
- Load all enabled profiles via ProfileRepo.list_enabled()
- Collect unique park_id, map_id pairs across all profiles (resolve park/map queries against BC Parks catalog at start of each poll cycle)
- For each unique pair, sequentially: call BCParksApi.map_availability() once, then for each profile watching this park: run pattern explosion + availability matching + prefer-longest dedup. If matches found, notify via Telegram immediately (per-profile tg_allowed_ids), then proceed to next profile
- After all profiles for this park are processed, proceed to next park
- SearchNotifier is created per profile (each gets its own rest_days and drive_times config)

Daemon refactor:
- daemon.py loads ProfileRepo and passes list of enabled profiles to Poller
- Removes load_profile() call (no more profile.json)

Migration:
- On Poller startup (or first profile command), if profile.json exists and no profiles in DB: read profile.json, create a profile named 'default' with all fields populated from the JSON, delete profile.json
- This is a function called at daemon startup and before any profile command

The old application/profile.py load_profile and _make_default_profile are removed.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Poller.run_search_once() uses ProfileRepo.list_enabled() instead of a single Profile
- [ ] Unique park grouping: 2 profiles watching same park → API called once
- [ ] Sequential processing: park A → notify profiles → park B
- [ ] Per-profile notification: each profile's tg_allowed_ids receives only its matches
- [ ] SearchNotifier created per profile with profile-specific rest_days and drive_times
- [ ] Daemon loads profiles from ProfileRepo, not profile.json
- [ ] profile.json migration: exists + no DB profiles → create 'default' profile, delete JSON
- [ ] Migration called on daemon startup
- [ ] Old load_profile / _make_default_profile removed
- [ ] Unit tests: 2 profiles same park (dedup), 2 profiles different parks (no dedup), disabled profile skipped, empty profile list
- [ ] Integration test: daemon starts, migrates profile.json, polls correctly

## Blocked by
Profile children: 7e85bx3w

name: [XS] profile-repo-cleanup-nits
description:
tech-debt from core-profile-repo-and-cli review (m15ten15):

1. cli.py:20 — drop `Profile` from `from ..application.profile import Profile, load_profile` (shadowed by domain.models.Profile import on line 28). Keep only `load_profile` until hb19863q removes it.

2. domain/models.py — `Profile.created_at` is `str | None` but schema is NOT NULL. Tighten or accept as testability artifact.

3. tests/test_profile_repo.py — add `test_extra_field_rejected` asserting Profile rejects unknown fields.

4. store.py — clock stamping currently in infrastructure; ADR-0007 wants application layer. Move into profile_service when slice 7e85bx3w introduces use-case functions.

5. cli.py profile commands use SqliteStore directly; switch to ProfileRepo Protocol injection when use-case layer lands.

Tests must still pass: `.venv/bin/pytest`.

name: [M] core-profile-repo-and-cli
description:
## Parent
[L] multi-profile

## What to build
Create the profiles table, Profile domain model, ProfileRepo protocol, and CLI commands for profile create/list/show/enable/disable/delete. This is the foundational slice — the Profile entity moves from application/profile.py (singleton JSON model) to domain/models.py (domain entity), persisted via a new ProfileRepo protocol implemented in SqliteStore.

Schema: profiles table with id, name, max_horizon_months, max_drive_hours, min_start_date, rest_days_between_bookings, enabled, created_at, updated_at.

ProfileRepo protocol: create, list_all, list_enabled, get_by_name, update, delete, set_enabled. Implementation in SqliteStore following existing patterns (Row-based deserialization).

CLI: campcli profile create <name>, profile list, profile show <name>, profile enable/disable <name>, profile delete <name>. The create command is interactive (prompts for parks, patterns, telegram IDs in a follow-up slice).

NOTE: This slice does NOT add child tables (patterns, parks, telegram) — only the core profiles table. Profile.children fields return empty lists.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] profiles table created with full schema (no child tables yet)
- [ ] Profile domain model in domain/models.py with all fields
- [ ] ProfileRepo protocol in domain/ports.py with create, list_all, list_enabled, get_by_name, update, delete, set_enabled
- [ ] SqliteStore implements ProfileRepo methods
- [ ] profile create <name> command creates a profile in the DB
- [ ] profile list command shows all profiles with enabled/disabled status
- [ ] profile show <name> command dumps full profile config
- [ ] profile enable/disable <name> toggles the enabled flag
- [ ] profile delete <name> removes the profile row
- [ ] Unit tests for ProfileRepo with in-memory SQLite
- [ ] Unit tests for profile service use-case functions
- [ ] Integration tests for CLI profile commands

## Blocked by
Cleanup: s9fwsg4i
result: Added profiles table (id, name UNIQUE, max_horizon_months, max_drive_hours, min_start_date, rest_days_between_bookings, enabled, created_at, updated_at). Added Profile Pydantic entity in domain/models.py with empty children lists. Added ProfileRepo Protocol in domain/ports.py with create/list_all/list_enabled/get_by_name/update/delete/set_enabled. Implemented in SqliteStore with Row-based deserialization and clock-based timestamps. Added 6 CLI sub-commands: profile create (interactive, child prompts TODO), profile list/show/enable/disable/delete. Tests: 187 passed (29 new for repo + CLI). Reviewer-fast PASS with 5 cosmetic tech-debt nits filed as separate task. 679 lines added, 4 removed across 7 files. Commit: 2be487d.

commit.message: feat: core Profile domain entity, ProfileRepo, and CLI sub-commands

name: [L] multi-profile
description:
Support multiple search profiles. Each profile has its own parks, patterns, and telegram notification targets. The crawl process is optimized: overlapping park watches across profiles trigger a single API crawl, with results fanned out to all interested profiles. Profiles are stored in the SQLite DB (state.db), not in profile.json. A CLI will manage profile CRUD (create, list, edit, delete, switch). Current state: single Profile singleton loaded from ~/.campcli/profile.json.
Grilled: yes
PRD: .scratch/multi-profile/PRD.md
Base: f7ef965c33e88af282af316f00bf38ec0888b119

name: [S] cleanup-watches-bookings-blocked
description:
## Parent
[L] multi-profile

## What to build
Drop the watches, bookings, and blocked_parks tables and all associated code. Remove domain models (Watch, Booking, BlockedPark), port protocols (WatchRepo, BookingRepo, BlockedParkRepo), application services (watches.py, bookings.py, blocked.py), CLI sub-commands (watch, bookings, blocked), and corresponding SqliteStore methods. Update Poller to no longer accept booking_repo and blocked_repo. The settings table and SettingsRepo are retained unchanged.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] watches, bookings, blocked_parks tables dropped from DB schema
- [ ] Watch, Booking, BlockedPark domain models removed
- [ ] WatchRepo, BookingRepo, BlockedParkRepo protocols removed from domain/ports.py
- [ ] watches.py, bookings.py, blocked.py application services removed
- [ ] watch, bookings, blocked CLI sub-commands removed
- [ ] SqliteStore methods for these repos removed
- [ ] Poller no longer depends on booking_repo or blocked_repo
- [ ] All existing tests pass (updated for removals)

## Blocked by
None - can start immediately
result: Removed Watch/Booking/BlockedPark domain models, WatchRepo/BookingRepo/BlockedParkRepo protocols, watches.py/bookings.py/blocked.py services, watch/booking/blocked CLI sub-commands, and the three SQLite tables. Poller no longer takes booking_repo or blocked_repo. Booked-site adjacency now flows as list[date] instead of list[Booking]. Tests: 158 passed in 0.30s. Reviewer-fast PASS (3 cosmetic tech-debt nits filed as separate task). 18 files changed, +31/-649 lines. Commit: 913ab10.

commit.message: cleanup: drop watches, bookings, blocked_parks tables and all associated code
